zh.lifeSummary: 當Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon於1315年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾吉德維爾利時，他的父親Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr45歲，母親Gwenillian ferch Cydifor50歲。他於大約1346年與Margaret Cradock在威爾士韋斯特 威爾斯結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon于1315年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾吉德維爾利时，他的父亲Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr45岁，母亲Gwenillian ferch Cydifor50岁。他于大约1346年与Margaret Cradock在威尔士韋斯特 威爾斯结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Welsh (Pembrokeshire and Glamorgan) and Irish (of Welsh origin): from the Old Welsh personal name Catgucaun, Catguocawn, modernized as Cadwgon. It is composed of cad ‘battle’ + gogawn ‘honour, glory’. See also Wogan . The surname is stressed, like its Welsh original, on the penultimate syllable, hence its occasional development to Duggan .

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: "Fychan"
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon was born in 1315, in Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr, was 45 and his mother, Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, was 50. He married Margaret Cradock about 1346, in West Wales, Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon est né en 1315, à Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, avait 50 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Cradock environ 1346, à West Wales, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon 1315 in Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr 45 und seine Mutter Gwenillian ferch Cydifor 50. Er hat um 1346 in West Wales, Wales Margaret Cradock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon è nato il nel 1315 nel Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, aveva 50 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Cradock circa 1346, nel West Wales, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgonが1315年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアキドウエリーで生まれた時，父Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawrは45歳，母Gwenillian ferch Cydiforは50歳であった。約1346年にウェールズウエスト ワレスでMargaret Cradockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon(이)가 1315년 웨일스 카마테실르 키드웰이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwenillian ferch Cydifor(은)는 50세였다. 그는 대략 1346년 웨일스 웨스트 월스에서 Margaret Cradock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon nasceu em 1315, em Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, tinha 50 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Cradock aproximadamente 1346, em West Wales, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon родился в 1315 г. в местности кидвелли, кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Cradock около 1346 г. в местности вест веилесь, Уэльс. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hywel "Fychan" ap Cadwgon nació en 1315, en Kidwelly, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Gwenillian ferch Cydifor, tenía 50 años. Se casó con Margaret Cradock aproximadamente 1346, en West Wales, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
